title: Third leopard captured in Jorhat within a week amid rising sightings - The Assam Tribune
full_text:
Third leopard captured in Jorhat within a week amid rising sightings
Asked about the rise in leopard sightings, the official attributed it to an increase in the leopard population following breeding.
Guwahati, July 8: A male leopard that had been straying into human settlements and preying on livestock was captured by the Forest Department at Tilikiam Tea Estate in western Jorhat on Wednesday, marking the third leopard to be trapped in the district within a week.
The leopard had reportedly created panic among residents by attacking domestic animals, including cattle and goats. Following repeated reports of its movement, the Jorhat Forest Division installed a cage in the area, where the animal was trapped in the early hours of Wednesday.
"Today we successfully captured the animal. It is a male leopard, estimated to be around seven to eight years old. Our staff had been working continuously to trap it," a Forest Department official said.
Asked about the rise in leopard sightings, the official attributed it to an increase in the leopard population following breeding.
"We have noticed more leopard sightings in recent times. They have been giving birth, which has led to an increase in their population. In some areas, they have created panic, although we have not received many complaints," the official said.
Notably, on July 4, a female leopard was also trapped from the same tea estate.
The captured male leopard has been shifted to the Jorhat Regional Forest Office for observation.
Forest officials said the animal appeared exhausted due to the prevailing heatwave. It has been cooled by pouring water over its body and is being kept in a shaded enclosure. The leopard will be released into a suitable forest habitat after receiving approval from senior authorities, they added.
The latest capture marks the third leopard to be trapped in Jorhat within a week, highlighting the increasing movement of leopards into human habitations in the district.
